original_hypothesis.title: USD price proposal cannot execute when APPROVE_THRESHOLD == 1 because proposeUSDPrice never calls _execUSDPriceUpdate() after auto-approval
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: proposeUSDPrice increments approvalsCount and records the proposer approval, but does not call _execUSDPriceUpdate(). Therefore, even if APPROVE_THRESHOLD is set to 1, the proposal will not be executed until a separate approveUSDPrice call occurs, effectively making the minimum approvals 2 and breaking admin-configured governance semantics.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is an explicit logic defect affecting governance behavior. While not an attacker fund-drain, it can break operations if the admin intentionally sets threshold to 1 for emergency updates (or accidentally sets it).
deployment_assumptions: Admin sets APPROVE_THRESHOLD to 1 via setUSDThresholds
Protocol expects immediate execution on proposal creation when threshold is 1
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Thresholds are settable via setUSDThresholds(uint8,uint8) with no bound checks in LockManager.

proof_of_signal.pattern: proposeUSDPrice sets approvalsCount++ but does not call _execUSDPriceUpdate()

preconditions: A price feed operator calls proposeUSDPrice

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Auto-approval not followed by threshold check/execution
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Operational emergency price updates may be blocked
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No immediate execution path in proposeUSDPrice

analysis_questions: Is APPROVE_THRESHOLD ever intended to be 1 in production or only for tests?
Do any deployment/config scripts set thresholds to 1?
Would an operator be incentivized to withhold the second approval to freeze prices?
validation_requirements: Unit test: set threshold to 1, propose, verify no update occurs until approveUSDPrice called
Check for any tooling that assumes 1 means immediate execution

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If threshold is set to 1 for operational reasons, any single operator can prevent updates by not sending the second approve tx; usdPrice remains stale, impacting rewards.
prerequisites: Threshold misconfiguration or emergency mode reliance

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Indirect: stale price feeds distort reward/weighting calculations
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users relying on usdPrice-integrated flows
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: High (can restore threshold or have another operator approve)

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Observe proposal remaining active with approvalsCount==1 and no state changes

validation_result.mechanism: missing threshold check after auto-approval in proposeUSDPrice
validation_result.impact_summary: When APPROVE_THRESHOLD is set to 1, proposeUSDPrice auto-approves (approvalsCount=1) but never checks the threshold or calls _execUSDPriceUpdate(). The proposer is also blocked from calling approveUSDPrice (ProposerCannotApproveError), so a second distinct operator must always approve, making the effective minimum 2 regardless of threshold setting.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The proposeUSDPrice function increments approvalsCount to 1 and records the proposer's approval, but contains no logic to check whether the threshold has been met or to call _execUSDPriceUpdate(). The _execUSDPriceUpdate() function is exclusively called from approveUSDPrice (line ~194). Meanwhile, approveUSDPrice explicitly blocks the proposer from calling it (line ~183: `if (usdUpdateProposal.proposer == msg.sender) revert ProposerCannotApproveError()`). Therefore, when APPROVE_THRESHOLD == 1, the proposer's auto-approval satisfies the threshold numerically but the execution path is never triggered. A different operator must call approveUSDPrice, bringing approvalsCount to 2, before _execUSDPriceUpdate fires. This means the minimum effective approval count is always 2 (proposer + 1 approver), breaking the governance semantics when admin intentionally sets APPROVE_THRESHOLD to 1 for emergency or streamlined price updates.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The defect is clearly visible in the source code. proposeUSDPrice (lines ~142-168) sets approvalsCount++ but has no threshold check or _execUSDPriceUpdate() call. approveUSDPrice (lines ~170-200) is the only function that calls _execUSDPriceUpdate(), and it blocks the proposer from calling it. setUSDThresholds (lines ~135-142) allows setting APPROVE_THRESHOLD to any uint8 value including 1 or 0, with no minimum bound validation.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: After incrementing approvalsCount to 1, the function emits an event and returns without checking if approvalsCount >= APPROVE_THRESHOLD or calling _execUSDPriceUpdate(). When APPROVE_THRESHOLD == 1, this means the threshold is met but execution never occurs.

This is the only function that calls _execUSDPriceUpdate(), but it blocks the proposer from calling it (ProposerCannotApproveError). So when APPROVE_THRESHOLD=1, the proposer cannot trigger execution despite having met the threshold.
No minimum bound validation on _approve. Admin can set APPROVE_THRESHOLD to 1 (or even 0), but the proposeUSDPrice function will never trigger execution regardless of how low the threshold is.

calling_sequence: 4. Function returns without checking threshold or calling _execUSDPriceUpdate()
5. Proposal sits in pending state with approvalsCount=1
6. Proposer cannot call approveUSDPrice due to ProposerCannotApproveError guard
7. A different operator must call approveUSDPrice to trigger _execUSDPriceUpdate()
8. Effective minimum approvals is always 2 regardless of APPROVE_THRESHOLD setting
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: proposeUSDPrice sets approvalsCount=1 at line ~170 but has no subsequent threshold check. The only _execUSDPriceUpdate() call is at line ~194 inside approveUSDPrice. approveUSDPrice blocks the proposer at line ~183. Therefore with APPROVE_THRESHOLD=1, execution requires at minimum the proposer (auto-approve in proposeUSDPrice) plus one additional approver (in approveUSDPrice).

deployment_assumptions_checked: Admin sets APPROVE_THRESHOLD to 1 via setUSDThresholds
Protocol expects immediate execution on proposal creation when threshold is 1
config_validation.deployment_evidence: Deployment scripts do not set USD thresholds explicitly. The default value is 3 (hardcoded in contract state). The setUSDThresholds function has no minimum bound checks, so threshold=1 is technically possible at any time. No deployment script contradicts or confirms the use of threshold=1.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The scenario is valid: if admin sets APPROVE_THRESHOLD=1 (for emergency price updates or any other reason), the proposeUSDPrice function will not auto-execute despite the threshold being met by the proposer's auto-approval. This requires an additional operator to call approveUSDPrice, defeating the purpose of a threshold of 1. While the default threshold is 3 (making this a non-issue in default config), the code explicitly allows the admin to set it to 1 through setUSDThresholds, and the behavior would be incorrect if they did.
validation_result.impact_assessment: Medium severity. The defect breaks governance semantics when APPROVE_THRESHOLD is configured to 1. Price updates that should execute immediately upon proposal would require a second operator, potentially causing delayed price updates in emergency scenarios. If only one operator is available (e.g., emergency situation), the price update is completely blocked. Stale prices can distort getLockedWeightedValue calculations, affecting reward distribution for all users.
recommended_mitigations: Add a threshold check at the end of proposeUSDPrice: if (usdUpdateProposal.approvalsCount >= APPROVE_THRESHOLD) { _execUSDPriceUpdate(); }
Add minimum bound validation in setUSDThresholds to enforce APPROVE_THRESHOLD >= 2 if the design intent is that proposals always require at least proposer + 1 approver
Alternatively, document that APPROVE_THRESHOLD represents additional approvals beyond the proposer, and adjust the threshold check in approveUSDPrice accordingly
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy LockManager with a mock configStorage. 2. Call setUSDThresholds(1, 3) as admin. 3. Call proposeUSDPrice with valid price and contracts from PriceFeed_1 role. 4. Verify usdUpdateProposal.approvalsCount == 1 and configuredTokens[token].usdPrice is NOT updated. 5. Verify PriceFeed_1 cannot call approveUSDPrice (ProposerCannotApproveError). 6. Call approveUSDPrice from PriceFeed_2 and verify price is now updated. 7. This proves minimum 2 approvals are needed regardless of APPROVE_THRESHOLD=1.
uncertainties: Whether the admin would ever intentionally set APPROVE_THRESHOLD to 1 in production (default is 3)
Whether the design intent is that APPROVE_THRESHOLD represents total approvals including proposer, or additional approvals beyond proposer
additional_testing_needed: Test with APPROVE_THRESHOLD=0 to see if proposeUSDPrice still requires at least 2 approvals
Test the interaction with DISAPPROVE_THRESHOLD when set to extreme values (0, 255)
